content: When a medication error occurs: (1) Assess patient immediately for adverse effects; (2) Stabilize if harm has occurred; (3) Notify prescriber and pharmacy; (4) Document error details in medical record (not blame-focused); (5) Report through institutional incident reporting system; (6) Disclose to patient/family per institutional policy; (7) Implement corrective actions to prevent recurrence. Prevention strategies include: computerized physician order entry (CPOE) with clinical decision support, barcode medication administration, standardized concentrations, tall man lettering for look-alike drugs, independent double-checks for high-alert medications, medication reconciliation at transitions, and creating a culture of safety that encourages reporting without fear of punishment.
